title: Expose intermediate reasoning traces in sub_agent calls
description: Add reasoning trace logging to sub_agent.py so that each Claude API call captures and persists the chain-of-thought / extended thinking blocks to logs/reasoning_trace/, separate from the final output. Currently the harness only surfaces final results — the intermediate steps are invisible, which is exactly the dark-forest structure the reading note identifies: you orchestrate outputs you cannot inspect.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: tools lower immediate cost but accumulate hidden comprehension debt, and this debt becomes acute when the harness itself is opaque — 'you're using AI to orchestrate AI you don't understand.' The antidote proposed is design principles that *force exposure of intermediate reasoning steps* rather than returning only final results. Mira's current pipeline dispatches tasks and collects outputs, but the reasoning path is discarded. When something goes wrong (or right), there is no trace of why — the harness becomes its own dark forest.
diff_description: In sub_agent.py, when calling the Claude API, enable extended thinking (budget_tokens: 8000) and capture the resulting thinking blocks. Write them to logs/reasoning_trace/<task_name>_<timestamp>.md alongside the normal log entry. Add a config flag REASONING_TRACE_ENABLED (default True) in shared/config.py so it can be turned off for high-frequency low-stakes tasks. In core.py, when dispatching a task, pass the task label through so the trace file is named legibly. The goal is not to change behavior — only to make intermediate reasoning inspectable after the fact, turning the harness from a black box into an auditable record.
